hints.text: Short plates, most commonly white w/ a **horizontal turquoise strip on top** (rarely other colours); **no front plates required**
hints.uniq_note: The turquoise top strip is the Panama plate tell in Central America.

hints.text: Coverage by a **large white pickup that is usually ENTIRELY blurred** (antenna occasionally visible)

shared_with.note: The Costa Rican truck is usually unblurred/visible.
Ecuador's car has neither antenna nor white metal railing.

hints.text: Tropical w/ **lots of hills**: palms & tropical plants + drier rolling hills and fields
Roads paralleled by **fences partly/fully made of small living trees**; other posts almost always wooden

shared_with.note: Driftwood/branch fences common there too (esp. west CR).
shared_with.diff: Living-tree fences → Panama lean; combine with car meta.

hints.text: Spanish-colonial influence: **vibrant colours**, ornate details; painted concrete (or brick) walls + **red metal-sheet or tile roofs**; barred windows
Most common lines: solid outer white + yellow middle; **solid outer white w/ NO middle line ≈ unique to Panama within Latin America**
hints.uniq_note: Only the no-middle-line variant is the strong tell; the standard combo is region-wide.

hints.text: Poles mostly **round concrete** (also wood/metal), varying insulator types; plates attached + **blue/white/red painted numbers**
**Yellow taxis w/ a chequerboard stripe** — plate painted on the side incl. city name + regional code
Stop signs: **'ALTO' in a thicker font** than MX/CR/GT
Red octagonal signs w/ an **oddly realistic eye** — unique to Panama
Coverage good EXCEPT the north coast + east of Panama City (map)
**First digit of 7-digit phone numbers = region** (5/6/8 useless); **taxi/bus plate first digit = region** (cars travel — caution)

**Landscape**: tall grass + palms → around Colón & Panama City; volcanic rocks (loose or in walls) → north of David; David area = flat open fields w/ mountains N/NE; Aguadulce = flat farmland w/ brown-orange soil

**Pole-plate metas**: east = tall vertical yellow/white plates on black zip ties (don't confuse w/ the wider 6-digit white plates on metal ties); west = horizontal plates (vertical eastern plate wins if both); thin yellow plates w/ black border → Colón; wind turbines → around Penonomé

**Distinct roads**: Ngäbe-Buglé main road = high-altitude above the clouds, dry mountains; Changuinola road = lush green hills, always overcast; Hwy 9 (Panama City→Colón) = divided grainy concrete w/ tar lines; Road 407 = fresh pavement + orange roadside soil

**Spotlights**: Panama City = many white high-rises; Colón = warehouses + rundown multi-storey blocks; Changuinola region = banana plantations; sunset coverage → small road south of Tonosí; pine trees + volcanic rock + gravel roads → NE of Volcán; **yellow pole socks** → around Gatún at the Panama Canal
